title: Get Tough! How to Win in Hand-to-Hand Fighting
authors: published by Paladin Press William E. Fairbairn
description: Get Tough! How to Win in Hand-to-Hand Fighting is a instructional by published by Paladin Press William E. Fairbairn. Confiscated by the Munich Regional Court in July 1991.
descriptionParagraphs: Get Tough! How to Win in Hand-to-Hand Fighting is a instructional by published by Paladin Press William E. Fairbairn. Confiscated by the Munich Regional Court in July 1991.

The surviving record is interesting because it shows how even ordinary-looking books can acquire a charged political afterlife. As a instructional, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in List of books confiscated for violating Criminal Code 86, 86a, 130 or 130a. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: Confiscated by the Munich Regional Court in July 1991. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

banningParagraphs: Get Tough! How to Win in Hand-to-Hand Fighting entered censorship debates as a instructional associated with controversy, publication history, and state scrutiny. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around political sensitivity.

The earliest event currently captured here is Date not yet pinned down in Germany, where List of books confiscated for violating Criminal Code 86, 86a, 130 or 130a authorities banned publication or circulation. counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
